theme: Review #100 — The office earned its soul. Now give it a heartbeat you can feel across tabs, screens, and standup links.

ideas.title: Browser Tab Favicon & Title Pulse
ideas.description: The browser tab is dead real estate. Dynamically update document.title to show vibe + active count: '🟢 3 active · Crushing It — Bloberto Office' or '🔴 ERROR · On Fire — Bloberto Office'. When vibe changes, briefly cycle a colored favicon (a tiny colored circle canvas-generated). WHY: Power users keep this tab pinned. Right now the tab says the same static title whether everything's on fire or the team just shipped. A pulsing tab title is the cheapest 'notification' system we'll ever build — zero infrastructure, maximum awareness. People WILL notice '🔴 ERROR' in their tab bar.

ideas.title: Daily Summary Tooltip on Stat Cards
ideas.description: Each stat card (Total, Active, Idle, Errors, Vibe) should show a hover tooltip with a one-line daily summary: 'Today: 12 tasks completed, 3 errors, peak vibe: Crushing It at 2pm'. Compute from activity.json on load. WHY: The stats show RIGHT NOW but never TODAY. A manager opens the tab at 4pm and sees '1 active' — is that good? Bad? The daily summary provides context without adding visual clutter. It's the difference between a speedometer and a trip computer.

ideas.title: Worker Arrival & Departure Announcements
ideas.description: When a worker transitions from idle→working, show a tiny toast-style notification at the bottom of the office view: '🌙 Luna clocked in · Creative review #100'. When they finish (working→idle/done): '✅ Luna shipped · Creative review #100 (6m)'. Auto-dismiss after 4s. Stack up to 2. WHY: The activity log captures this but it's below the fold. The office view is where people WATCH — arrivals and departures should be felt in real-time, like hearing the office door open. It makes the office feel populated by people who come and go, not sprites that just appear.

ideas.title: Milestone Confetti for Review #100
ideas.description: Add a one-time confetti burst when the dashboard detects review_number >= 100 in ideas.json (or a milestone counter in activity). Show a brief banner: '🎉 100th Creative Review!' with confetti. The confetti system already exists — just trigger it on a data condition. WHY: We already HAVE confetti code. Luna's 100th review is a team milestone. Celebrating it makes the dashboard feel like it has memory and awareness of its own history. It costs nothing and it makes people smile exactly once.

ideas.title: Conference Table Meeting Visualization
ideas.description: The conference table SVG exists but meetings feel empty. When 2+ workers are idle simultaneously, show them as tiny seated avatars around the table with a 'STANDUP' or 'SYNC' label. Use the existing .char--seated class. WHY: The conference table is gorgeous furniture in an empty room. When 3 workers finish their tasks and go idle, they should visually congregate — it tells a story of collaboration without a single line of narrative text. The CSS classes already exist (.char--seated, .conf-meeting-badge). This is mostly wiring.

ideas.title: Keyboard Command Palette
ideas.description: Press '/' or Cmd+K to open a minimal command palette: 'Switch to Office / Dashboard', 'Jump to Luna', 'Toggle theme', 'Refresh data'. 6-8 commands max. Filter as you type. WHY: The shortcut toast already teaches users that keyboard shortcuts exist. A command palette is the natural evolution — it's the power-user entry point. It also makes the dashboard feel like a real product, not a toy. Every serious tool has one now. Keep it tiny — 8 commands, fuzzy match, done.

ideas.title: Bloberto Daily Mood Journal
ideas.description: Bloberto's thought bubble currently picks from static quips. Add a 'journal mode' where clicking Bloberto in the office view shows a small modal with his daily observations: 'Luna crushed 7 ideas today. Carlos fixed 4 bugs. The vibe peaked at Crushing It around 11am.' Generated from activity.json data. WHY: Bloberto is the soul of the office but he's a one-liner machine. A daily journal gives him depth — he's not just quipping, he's OBSERVING. It turns the manager character from decoration into documentation. And it's sharable — 'look what my AI manager wrote about today'.

ideas.title: Ambient Office Soundscape
ideas.description: Optional (off by default, toggle in header) ambient office sounds: gentle keyboard typing when workers are active, a coffee machine gurgle when someone visits the coffee corner, a door creak on arrivals. Use Web Audio API with tiny base64-encoded samples. Total payload under 50KB. WHY: The office is SILENT. You watch it, you don't hear it. Sound is the sense we've completely ignored. Even 3 subtle sounds would transform the experience from 'watching a screensaver' to 'sitting in an office'. Off by default respects users. But when someone turns it on... they'll never turn it off.
